options: both — must catch silent filesystem drops too
decision_log.choice: both — must catch silent filesystem drops too
decision_log.rationale: Users will drop files into directories without telling the agent; the system must detect drift even when no announcement is made.

options: leave PreToolUse hook intact and carve a separate human-writable zone (uploads, replaceable outputs)
relax the hook so humans can also overwrite units/feedback/intent.md/state.json directly
decision_log.choice: leave PreToolUse hook intact and carve a separate human-writable zone (uploads, replaceable outputs)
decision_log.rationale: SPA / review-app / direct-filesystem edits are out-of-band human actions, not agent writes — hooks remain the agent boundary, not a human boundary. State-machine-managed files stay MCP-only at the agent level.

options: new workflow action manual_change_assessment with four agent-classified outcomes
piggyback on the feedback-triage gate by synthesizing FBs from the differ
decision_log.choice: new workflow action manual_change_assessment with four agent-classified outcomes
decision_log.rationale: Provenance matters — piggybacking on FB-triage loses the "this came from outside the system" signal. Distinct action lets the agent route to ignore / inline-fix / surface-as-FB / trigger-revisit explicitly.

options: lock the workflow during human edits
eventual consistency — next haiku_run_next tick observes drift and reacts
decision_log.choice: eventual consistency — next haiku_run_next tick observes drift and reacts
decision_log.rationale: Locking introduces coordination cost the framework doesn't currently have. Eventual consistency is acceptable; mid-bolt concurrency may produce partial-state work but the next tick reconciles.

decision_log.decision: Scope of change-types covered in v1
options: just stage-output replacement and knowledge upload (1 + 3)
all three: output replacement, PO 1-line edit + AI extension, knowledge upload
decision_log.choice: all three: output replacement, PO 1-line edit + AI extension, knowledge upload
decision_log.rationale: All three motivating workflows are real; the agent's classification step is the unifier so a single mechanism (`manual_change_assessment`) covers them via different outcomes.
Out-of-band human writes are a real lifecycle concept, not a plugin implementation detail — it deserves a paper section, plugin implementation, and user-facing docs.
All three entry points reduce to the same on-disk drift the implicit baseline gate observes; the next run_next tick reconciles regardless of how the file got there.
